claimText: The National Quantum Mission (NQM) is implemented by the Department of Science and Technology (DST).
evidenceExcerpt: NQM is implemented by the Department of Science and Technology (DST).
examUse: Statement-based question on nodal ministries for national missions.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)

claimText: The National Quantum Mission is one of the nine initiatives under the Prime Minister's Science, Technology, and Innovation Advisory Council (PMSTIAC).
evidenceExcerpt: It is implemented by the Department of Science and Technology and is one of the nine PMSTIAC initiatives.
examUse: Statement-based question on the origin and umbrella framework of the mission.

claimText: The National Quantum Mission aims to develop quantum computers with a 1,000-qubit capacity and a 2,000 km secure communication network.
evidenceExcerpt: Aims to develop quantum computers with 1,000 qubits capacity and a 2,000 km communication network.
examUse: Statement-based question testing specific targets of the National Quantum Mission.

claimText: The National Quantum Mission plans to establish four thematic hubs (T-Hubs) dedicated to computing, communication, sensing, and materials.
evidenceExcerpt: Plans to establish four thematic hubs (T-Hubs) in computing, communication, sensing, and materials.
examUse: Institution-feature question on the structural components of the National Quantum Mission.
distractorHooks: Hubs dedicated to Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain

claimText: QpiAI-Indus is India's first 25-qubit superconducting quantum computer.
evidenceExcerpt: QpiAI launched India's first 25-qubit superconducting quantum computer, QpiAI-Indus.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching the name of the technology (QpiAI-Indus) with its description.
distractorHooks: A 1000-qubit quantum computer developed by C-DAC
